weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a flawless blue, the kind of clear sky that sharpens edges and invites longer strides. Through late morning into the early afternoon, sun rules the day with little to soften it; shadows stay crisp, the air looks clean, and visibility feels almost polished. UV will climb quickly around midday despite the season’s gentler lean, so a brimmed hat, sunglasses, and a slick of sunscreen are sensible companions. Light layers will suit the warmth that builds under full sun; carry water and let shade be your ally when you pause.

It’s the sort of day for ordinary pleasures done unhurried: walk to your errands instead of driving, read a few pages at a café table tucked under an awning, hang a fresh wash to dry, or give the garden a slow, attentive watering. If you need an excuse to step out, return that library book and take the long way back; the sky is doing its best work and won’t be interrupted soon.
